2604.17377.title: AnchorMem: Anchored Facts with Associative Contexts for Building Memory in Large Language Models
2604.17377.paper_summary: While large language models have achieved remarkable performance in complex tasks, they still need a memory system to utilize historical experience in long-term interactions. Existing memory methods (e.g., A-Mem, Mem0) place excessive emphasis on organizing interactions by frequently rewriting them, however, this heavy reliance on summarization risks diluting essential contextual nuances and obscuring key retrieval features. To bridge this gap, we introduce AnchorMem, a novel memory framework inspired by the Proust Phenomenon in cognitive science, where a specific anchor triggers a holistic recollection. We propose a method that decouples the retrieval unit from the generation context. AnchorMem extracts atomic facts from interaction history to serve as retrieval anchors, while preserving the original context as the immutable context. To reveal implicit narrative cues, we construct an associative event graph that uses higher-order event links that bind sets of related facts into shared event representations, strengthening cross-memory integration without relying on generic entities as bridges. During retrieval, the system anchors queries to specific facts and events to locate relevant memories, but reconstructs the context using the associated raw chunks and events. Our method reconciles fine-grained retrieval with the contextual integrity of interactions. Experiments across three closed-source and open-source models on the LoCoMo benchmark demonstrate that AnchorMem significantly outperforms baselines. Code is available at https://github.com/RayNeo-AI-2025/AnchorMem.
2604.17377.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型在複雜任務中取得了顯著的表現，但它們仍然需要一個記憶系統來利用長期互動中的歷史經驗。現有的記憶方法（例如 A-Mem、Mem0）過度強調通過頻繁重寫來組織互動，然而，這種對摘要的重度依賴風險在於稀釋了基本的上下文細微差別並模糊了關鍵檢索特徵。為了彌補這一差距，我們引入了 AnchorMem，一種新的記憶框架，靈感來自於認知科學中的普魯斯特現象，其中一個特定的錨點觸發整體的回憶。我們提出了一種方法，將檢索單元與生成上下文解耦。AnchorMem 從互動歷史中提取原子事實作為檢索錨點，同時保留原始上下文作為不變的上下文。為了揭示隱含的敘事線索，我們構建了一個聯想事件圖，使用高階事件鏈將一組相關事實綁定到共享事件表示中，增強跨記憶整合，而不依賴於通用實體作為橋樑。在檢索過程中，系統將查詢錨定到特定的事實和事件，以定位相關的記憶，但使用相關的原始片段和事件重建上下文。我們的方法調和了細緻的檢索與互動的上下文完整性。在 LoCoMo 基準上對三個封閉源和開源模型的實驗表明，AnchorMem 明顯優於基準。代碼可在 https://github.com/RayNeo-AI-2025/AnchorMem 獲得。
2604.17377.author: Zhanyu Shen et.al.
2604.17377.authors: Zhanyu Shen, Sijie Cheng, Zhicheng Guo, Weiqin Wang, Yile Wang, Hui Huang
